title: aconitum × hebegynum
intro: Aconitum × hebegynum, also known as Monk's Hood, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the Aconitum genus, a group of flowering plants known for their distinctive hood-shaped flowers and potential toxicity. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. In herbal remedies, Aconitum × hebegynum is often used to alleviate pain, reduce fever, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. Preparations of the plant include tinctures, infusions, and ointments, although caution must be taken when handling the plant due to its potential toxicity.
